pi_items.source: Task description and verified by independent calculation. Standard capacitor values (in microfarads) used for electrical engineering demonstration.

Calculated using series capacitor formula: 1/C_total = 1/100 + 1/220 + 1/470 = 0.01667311 → C_total = 59.9768 µF. Formula verified against multiple authoritative sources: Omnicalculator.com, DigiKey, Physics LibreTexts (OpenStax), and Electronics-tutorials.ws. All confirm: 1/C_total = 1/C1 + 1/C2 + 1/C3.

The calculated value (59.9768) is 0.00319 µF away from claimed value (59.98), well within the 0.5 µF tolerance margin. Tolerance value is reasonable for UI input tolerance and floating-point representation.
Task description explicitly states 'C1: 100 µF', 'C2: 220 µF', 'C3: 470 µF'. Unit is microfarads (µF), standard SI unit for capacitance.

Task description explicitly states: 'select the Series calculator' and 'Calculate the equivalent capacitance of three capacitors connected in series'. Verifier.py confirms by checking for 'Series' indicator and checking against parallel mistake (790 µF).

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and correct. The task requires calculating series capacitance for three capacitors (100 µF, 220 µF, 470 µF) using the series formula 1/C_total = 1/C1 + 1/C2 + 1/C3, yielding 59.9768 µF (displayed as 59.98 µF). The tolerance of 0.5 µF accommodates reasonable UI input variation. The data is entirely synthetic (hardcoded demonstration values). The verifier correctly detects common mistakes such as using the parallel formula (which would yield 790 µF).
